---
title: "A preliminary study of a camp-based dynamic contemplative course on children’s sustained attention and balance"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

## Method

A case-series pretest-posttest study with five children participating in a six-day camp-based BMAA dynamic contemplative course.

## Key Findings

- Two children showed clearer improvement in visual sustained attention, one in auditory sustained attention, and four in balance performance.
- Children with stronger attention gains also tended to show balance improvements.

## Reference

葉淨維, 連韻文, 陳人瑜, 莊博雅, 鄭惟馨, & 陳顥齡. (2023). 營隊式動態靜觀課程對兒童持續注意力與平衡能力影響之初探. *台灣職能治療研究與實務, 19*(1), 81-97.
